message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_02-31-52_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Activate test: "should apply -20% loyalty discount when customer has >= 2 years with MHPCO"
Current state: 18 tests passing
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_02-31-52_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

Spec context: Per spec: "Long-standing customers (≥ 2 years of business with MHPCO) receive a 20% loyalty discount." Per the policy modifier section: "policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up contract) apply to the policy base premium (the sum of all item base premiums)".

Wait - the spec says first-insurance is policy-wide too, but the integration example treats it per-item. Let me re-read.

Integration example for newcomer cursed sword: "100 G base + 50 G curse + 10 G first insurance" - the first insurance is 10% of 100 = 10G. With one item, both interpretations give same result.

But for "first insurance" the spec says: "each item in a quote is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer history." So first ins is per item.

For loyalty (-20%), the spec example "Long-standing customer's second contract: -20 G loyalty" — that's 20% of 100 base = 20. Hmm but that's also just one item.

Looking at "policy base premium (the sum of all item base premiums)" — loyalty discount is computed on the sum of bases.

For this isolated test: Customer with 2 years, no follow-up, sword (steel, ench 3):
- Base 100, first ins 10, loyalty -20% on base 100 = -20
- 100 - 20 + 10 + 5 fee = 95 G

Wait - is loyalty applied before or after first-ins? Let me try: 100 base, sum = 100, loyalty -20 = 80 (policy base after loyalty). First ins is per item: +10. Plus 5 fee = 95 G.

Or maybe: base 100, then first ins +10 (per item, adds to subtotal), then loyalty -20% on policy base which is the sum of bases (=100): -20. So 100 + 10 - 20 + 5 = 95 G.

Both interpretations give 95 G in this case. Use 95.

Scenario: {customer: {yearsWithMHPCO: 2}, steps: [{op: "quote", items: [{type: "sword", material: "steel", enchantment: 3, cursed: false}]}]}
Expected: {results: [{premium: 95}]}

Current code: ignores customer years → returns 115. Expected 95, received 115.

Run tests with: cd /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_02-31-52_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7 && pnpm test

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim (both Compilation
Prediction and Runtime Prediction lines, each with Correct or
Incorrect). Do not abbreviate. Do not collapse the two prediction
lines into one.

content.text: I'll execute the Red phase for the loyalty discount test.
